Test Description: Registers default-value and masked write/read verification across GPIO block using enumerated register arrays and per-register read/write masks. Verifies reset defaults and that writable bits accept patterns while read-only bits retain their default values.
Remarks:
- VRRW registers are skipped as indicated by skip_array comments.
- Note: When reading default values, DIN may float to 1 unless externally forced; forcing DIN to 0 can drive bit-level select high, causing mismatches (from source comment).
Test Steps / Procedure:
Runtime execution flow:
1) Entry: test_case() initializes local counters and calls chk_rst_val().
2) chk_rst_val():
   - For i from 0 to CNT-1 (CNT=49):
     a) addr = addr_array[i]. If skip_rst_array[i] == 1, continue to next i.
     b) If read_mask_array[i] == 0x00000000, continue to next i (address not readable).
     c) data_rd = read_reg(addr).
     d) data = (data_rd & 0xfffffffe) [masking off bit0 per source].
     e) Compare data with default_value_array[i]. If equal: PASS (optional DEBUG print). Else: def_fail_cnt++ and print failure with addr, expected, read.
3) Return to test_case(); proceed to chk_rd_wr().
4) chk_rd_wr():
   - Define test patterns chk_val[6] = {0xffffffff, 0xaaaaaaaa, 0x55555555, 0xf5f5f5f5, 0xA5A5A5A5, 0xffff0000}.
   - For each pattern j = 0..5:
     a) data_wr = chk_val[j].
     b) Write phase: For i = 0..CNT-1:
        i.   addr = addr_array[i]. If skip_array[i] == 1, continue.
        ii.  If write_mask_array[i] == 0x00000000, continue (not writable).
        iii. write_reg(addr, (data_wr & write_mask_array[i])). Optional DEBUG print of address and data.
     c) Read/verify phase: For i = 0..CNT-1:
        i.   addr = addr_array[i]. If skip_array[i] == 1, continue.
        ii.  If write_mask_array[i] == 0x00000000, continue (skip; not writable).
        iii. If read_mask_array[i] == 0x00000000, continue (skip; not readable).
        iv.  data_rd = (read_reg(addr) & read_mask_array[i]).
        v.   wr_n = (write_mask_array[i] ^ 0xffffffff).
        vi.  exp_val = ((data_wr & read_mask_array[i] & write_mask_array[i]) | (wr_n & read_mask_array[i] & default_value_array[i])).
        vii. If data_rd == exp_val: PASS (optional DEBUG print). Else: wr_fail_cnt++ and print failure with addr, expected, read.
5) Test completion: In test_case(), if (def_fail_cnt > 0 || wr_fail_cnt > 0) finish(1) else finish(0). No waits/delays are used in executed paths (soft_reset_chk is compiled out under #ifdef 0).
Validation / Acceptance Criteria:
- PASS if def_fail_cnt == 0 and wr_fail_cnt == 0 leading to finish(0).
- Any default value mismatch increments def_fail_cnt and is reported.
- Any write/read mismatch against computed exp_val increments wr_fail_cnt and is reported.
Gap Analysis: No negative tests identified; soft_reset_chk() is disabled (#ifdef 0).

Test Description: GPIO negedge interrupt enable/validation for pads 8..39: configures each pad for input + negedge detection, enables and tests one bit at a time by generating a falling edge, and validates per-pin raw and group status as well as system-level interrupt clear.
Remarks:
- Uses bounded wait with timeout (5000 iterations with wait_on(10)) to avoid infinite loop; timing base is environment-dependent (comment: adjust to sim time base if needed).
- Arms int_pend before generating the edge to avoid race conditions.
Test Steps / Procedure:
Runtime execution flow:
1) Entry: test_case() sets test_err=0.
2) If GPIO0 defined: GIC_EnableIRQ(87); If GPIO1 defined: GIC_EnableIRQ(88).
3) Enable sysreg interrupt output:
   - If GPIO0: write_reg(MIZAR_LSS_SYSREG_INTR_EN1, LSS_SYSREG_INTR_EN1_GPIO0_INTR).
   - If GPIO1: write_reg(MIZAR_LSS_SYSREG_INTR_EN1, LSS_SYSREG_INTR_EN1_GPIO1_INTR).
4) Initialize pad driver to known state high: write_reg(0xA0243ffc, 0xffffffff).
5) Configure pads 8..39 for input + negedge detect and clear any latched raw per pin:
   - For i = 0..31: addr1 = MIZAR_GPIO_GP0_GPIO_8 + (i*4); write_reg(addr1, (1<<20)|(1<<18)|(1<<16)); wait_on(10).
6) For i = 0..31 (test each pin individually):
   a) wr_val = (1u << i).
   b) Pre-clear group raw status bit: write_reg(MIZAR_GPIO_GPIO_INTR_RAW_STCLR1, wr_val).
   c) Enable only this bit: write_reg(MIZAR_GPIO_GP0_INTR1_INTR_EN1, wr_val); wait_on(10).
   d) Arm wait: int_pend = 1.
   e) Generate falling edge on this bit:
      - write_reg(0xA0243ffc, 0xffffffff); wait_on(30); write_reg(0xA0243ffc, ~wr_val).
   f) Poll with timeout: timeout=5000; while (int_pend && timeout--) wait_on(10).
      - If timeout == 0: print timeout error for pin (i+8), test_err++.
7) Completion: finish(test_err).
8) Default_IRQHandler():
   a) local_wr = (1u << i); int_pend = 0.
   b) Drive all pads high: write_reg(0xA0243ffc, 0xffffffff).
   c) raddr = MIZAR_GPIO_GP0_GPIO_8 + (i*4); rdata = read_reg(raddr).
   d) Validate falling-edge DIN: (rdata & 0x1) must be 0 else test_err++.
   e) If ((rdata & 0x2) != 0x0):
      - rdata_grp = read_reg(MIZAR_GPIO_GP0_INTR1_INTR_STS1); ensure (rdata_grp & local_wr) != 0 else test_err++.
      - Clear per-pin raw: write_reg(MIZAR_GPIO_GP0_GPIO_8 + (i*4), (1<<20)|(1<<16)).
      - Clear group raw: write_reg(MIZAR_GPIO_GPIO_INTR_RAW_STCLR1, local_wr).
      - Verify group clear: rdata_grp = read_reg(MIZAR_GPIO_GP0_INTR1_INTR_STS1); if (rdata_grp != 0) test_err++.
      - If GPIO0: write_reg(MIZAR_LSS_SYSREG_RAW_STCR1, LSS_SYSREG_RAW_STCR1_GPIO0_INTR); GIC_ClearIRQ(87).
        If GPIO1: write_reg(MIZAR_LSS_SYSREG_RAW_STCR1, LSS_SYSREG_RAW_STCR1_GPIO1_INTR); GIC_ClearIRQ(88).
     Else: test_err++ (raw bit not set).
Validation / Acceptance Criteria:
- No timeout should occur while waiting for ISR; any timeout increments test_err.
- In ISR: DIN bit (LSB) must be 0 after negedge; group status must indicate the tested bit; clearing per-pin raw and group raw must result in MIZAR_GPIO_GP0_INTR1_INTR_STS1 == 0.
- System-level RAW_STCR1 bit for the selected GPIO instance must be cleared after write.
- Test result: finish(test_err) with test_err==0 for PASS.
Gap Analysis: No negative tests identified (e.g., behavior with interrupts disabled not tested).

Test Description: GPIO posedge interrupt on all pads (8..39): enables posedge detect and group interrupt, sequences a single rising edge per pad, validates group interrupt assertion and full clear, and verifies system-level interrupt status clear.
Remarks:
- Masks group interrupt during ISR service to avoid re-entry.
- Uses bounded wait (timeout=2000 with wait_on(10)) to avoid infinite loops.
Test Steps / Procedure:
Runtime execution flow:
1) Entry: test_case(). If GPIO0: GIC_EnableIRQ(87); If GPIO1: GIC_EnableIRQ(88).
2) Enable sysreg interrupt:
   - If GPIO0: write_reg(MIZAR_LSS_SYSREG_INTR_EN1, LSS_SYSREG_INTR_EN1_GPIO0_INTR).
   - If GPIO1: write_reg(MIZAR_LSS_SYSREG_INTR_EN1, LSS_SYSREG_INTR_EN1_GPIO1_INTR).
3) Configure per-pin posedge detection: For i=0..31, write_reg(MIZAR_GPIO_GP0_GPIO_8 + i*4, 0x00020000) [peie=1].
4) wait_on(10).
5) Configure GPIOs 8..39 as inputs (doe=1) via group IO control:
   - write_reg(MIZAR_GPIO_GPIO_IO_CTRL_GROUP1, 0x000000FF);
   - write_reg(MIZAR_GPIO_GPIO_IO_CTRL_GROUP2, 0x000000FF);
   - write_reg(MIZAR_GPIO_GPIO_IO_CTRL_GROUP3, 0x000000FF);
   - write_reg(MIZAR_GPIO_GPIO_IO_CTRL_GROUP4, 0x000000FF).
6) wait_on(10).
7) Enable group interrupt for all bits: write_reg(MIZAR_GPIO_GP0_INTR1_INTR_EN1, 0xFFFFFFFF).
8) For i=0..31 (test each pad):
   a) Drive known low: write_reg(0xA0243ffc, 0x00000000); wait_on(10).
   b) Arm: int_pend = 1.
   c) Generate rising edge: write_reg(0xA0243ffc, 0xFFFFFFFF).
   d) Poll with timeout: timeout=2000; while (int_pend==1 && --timeout>0) wait_on(10).
      - If timeout==0: print error, test_err++, break.
   e) Drive low for next iteration: write_reg(0xA0243ffc, 0x00000000); wait_on(10).
9) finish(test_err).
10) Default_IRQHandler():
    a) wr_val = (1<<i); int_pend = 0.
    b) Read group status: rdata_grp = read_reg(MIZAR_GPIO_GP0_INTR1_INTR_STS1).
    c) Mask group enable during service: write_reg(MIZAR_GPIO_GP0_INTR1_INTR_EN1, 0x00000000).
    d) If rdata_grp != 0: success print; else: error and test_err++.
    e) Clear per-pin raw for all pads: for j=0..31 write_reg(MIZAR_GPIO_GP0_GPIO_8 + j*4, 0x00010000); wait_on(2).
    f) Verify group clear: rdata_grp = read_reg(MIZAR_GPIO_GP0_INTR1_INTR_STS1); if (rdata_grp != 0) error and test_err++.
    g) Clear sysreg RAW_STCR1 for instance: If GPIO0: write_reg(MIZAR_LSS_SYSREG_RAW_STCR1, LSS_SYSREG_RAW_STCR1_GPIO0_INTR) and verify bit cleared. If GPIO1: similarly with GPIO1 bit.
    h) Re-enable group interrupt: write_reg(MIZAR_GPIO_GP0_INTR1_INTR_EN1, 0xFFFFFFFF).
    i) Clear GIC IRQ: If GPIO0: GIC_ClearIRQ(87); If GPIO1: GIC_ClearIRQ(88).
Validation / Acceptance Criteria:
- Group interrupt must assert (non-zero MIZAR_GPIO_GP0_INTR1_INTR_STS1) during ISR.
- After clearing per-pin raw (iclr) across pads, group status must read 0.
- System RAW_STCR1 bit for selected GPIO instance must be cleared after write.
- finish(test_err) with test_err==0 indicates PASS.
Gap Analysis: No negative tests identified (e.g., disabled-interrupt behavior, debounce/noise not exercised).
